blockingIssues.description: The runtime still calls `fetchExchangeTickers` for every configured exchange on every refresh cycle with no circuit-breaker, cool-down, or fail-fast gate. This feature's stated scope required provider-failure protection such as circuit-breaker or fail-fast semantics, but the implementation only marks degraded state after a thrown refresh error. Repeated provider failures therefore continue to hit upstream providers instead of being short-circuited.

`readiness.state` returns `ready` whenever `initialSyncCompleted` is true, even if `allowStaleLiveService` or `syncFailureReason` still indicate degraded fallback. This makes `/diagnostics/runtime` report a recovered/healthy state while stale-live fallback and provider-failure indicators remain active, violating the machine-readable readiness/degraded contract and the feature's expected behavior.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented that the shared HTTP app test fixture completes bootstrap before inject, so true pre-ready startup state must be tested via runtime-state/diagnostics tests instead of route-level app.inject probes.
